task_description: Write a method in Dafny to compute the nth Fibonacci number.
verifier_error:

19 | }

primary_error.message: a postcondition could not be proved on this return path

repair_strategy.problem_summary: Missing loop invariants prevent Dafny from proving that the computed result equals fib(n).

problem_details.what: The while loop in method 'ComputeFib' lacks loop invariants and a decreases clause. Without these, Dafny cannot verify that the postcondition 'b == fib(n)' holds after the loop terminates.
problem_details.why: In Dafny, loops require explicit invariants to establish the relationship between loop variables and the specification. The variables 'b' and 'c' iteratively compute Fibonacci values, but without invariants stating 'b == fib(i)' and 'c == fib(i+1)', Dafny has no way to know what these variables represent at each iteration. When the loop exits with 'i == n', Dafny cannot deduce that 'b == fib(n)'.
problem_details.how: Add three loop invariants and a decreases clause: (1) 'decreases n - i' to prove loop termination, (2) 'invariant 0 < i <= n' to maintain index bounds, (3) 'invariant b == fib(i)' to establish that b holds the i-th Fibonacci number, and (4) 'invariant c == fib(i+1)' to establish that c holds the (i+1)-th Fibonacci number. When the loop exits with i == n, the invariant 'b == fib(i)' directly implies the postcondition 'b == fib(n)'.
